highlights.text: Remember that dips are always bought via my core setup: The Failed Breakdown.
In this case, the Failed Breakdown of yesterdays daily low at 5607 would be the actionable setup for today and I wrote yesterday at 4pm: “Below 5620, we should see a new low to 5597-5600.

Instead of just buying this, a safer bet is to wait for the Failed Breakdown of today’s low which is just a little above at 5607.” At 4:40PM last night, we lost 5607, put in a Failed Breakdown of yesterdays 5607 low, and squeezed violently to 5689.

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into three quality setups we have had this week via my methodology: A Failed Breakdown on Monday (my core setup, which took us to Monday’s 5706 high) then a Breakdown Short (my third setup type, which caused yesterdays sell).

post_title: SPX Is Coiled Tight For A Huge Move. What Way? May 8 Plan
